授業目的: R1 (Reading 1) aims to develop practical reading skills by addressing the reading weaknesses unique to Japanese English learners. It also aims to help learners acquire and utilize reading skills and strategies that are particularly effective for Japanese learners. R2 (Reading 2) aims to enable fluent reading by further internalizing and automating the language knowledge and skills acquired in R1 through the use of extensive reading materials. W (Writing) aims to enable learners to construct English texts starting primarily at the paragraph level and eventually reaching the essay level. OC (Oral Communication) aims to teach basic phonetic abilities such as pronunciation, rhythm, and intonation, as well as basic skills for effective presentations such as gestures and eye contact, and strategies for maintaining conversations.
到達目標: R1 (Reading 1) primarily aims to strengthen the reading weaknesses unique to Japanese English learners. Specifically, it aims to enhance vocabulary and grammar skills, and to transform them from mere knowledge into practical skills that can be used in real-world situations, with training provided to achieve this. In addition, while engaging in close reading, it will also touch upon reading skills and strategies that are considered particularly effective for Japanese learners. Students will be able to read and summarize intermediate-level English texts (vocabulary level 1900-2200: B2 of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages). R2 (Reading 2) primarily aims to provide opportunities to enjoyably expose students to a wide range of English through the use of extensive reading materials. The goal is to achieve fluent reading by further internalizing and automating the language knowledge and application skills acquired in R1. Students will be able to fluently read Graded Readers level 2 and above. W (Writing) aims to develop English writing skills, starting mainly at the paragraph level and ultimately reaching the essay level. Students will be able to write narratives of 250-500 words and essays of 300-750 words. Oral Communication (OC) training will focus on effective listening and speaking. Basic phonetics instruction, including pronunciation, rhythm, and intonation, will be provided, along with the development of essential presentation skills such as gestures and eye contact. Furthermore, students will learn strategies for maintaining conversations. Students will be able to converse on familiar and simple topics and participate in small group discussions.
授業方法: R1: It consists of intensive reading using textbooks appropriate for the students' level. Students will achieve understanding of relevant vocabulary using AWL. R2: It consists of extensive reading activities using graded reader books in the KGU library, group work, and presentation. W: Blog writing. Reading model essays. Studying modes of academic essays. Timed writing practice. Writing two academic essays per semester All instruction takes place in English. OC: Lecture style instruction (English only), Group/Pair Discussion, Presentation making This course will be entirely conducted face-to-face.

トピック.授業外学習2: いずれの授業も受講生が主体的、積極的に参加することを前提としたものである。受講生は周到な準備の上、主体的積極的な姿勢で授業に臨むこと。

教科書/Required texts0:

Textbook information will be given on the first day of teaching.
学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic:
各授業科目は、各学部・研究科の定めるディプロマ・ポリシー（DP）・カリキュラム・ポリシー（CP）に基づき、カリキュラム上に配置されています。
DP・CPを意識して本授業科目の学修を進めることで、各学部・研究科の期待する以下の能力が養成されます。
言語教育科目：１．言語コミュニケーション能力：外国語力を生かし、積極的に発信できる。２．異文化理解・多文化共生力：異文化に対する感受性をもち、多様な文化と共生できる。
